text: Code reviews are an important part of software development. They involve team members checking each other's code before it is added to the main project. One major benefit is catching bugs early, which saves time and money compared to fixing them later. Code reviews also encourage knowledge sharing, as developers learn different techniques and solutions from their colleagues. This helps spread expertise across the team and prevents over-reliance on a single person. Ultimately, regular code reviews improve overall team quality by creating a culture of collaboration and continuous improvement. They lead to cleaner, more reliable code and stronger, more confident developers.
